2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"testing"
6	
7		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
8		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
9	)

11	// TestBdSchemaCompatibility verifies that DDx bead JSONL output matches the
12	// bd (Dolt-backed bead tracker) schema exactly. This test locks in the field
13	// names so we never accidentally diverge from bd/br interchange format.
14	func TestBdSchemaCompatibility(t *testing.T) {
15		// This is a real bd export line (from `bd export --format jsonl`)
16		bdExport := `{"id":"bd-test-1x4","title":"test issue","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"user@example.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:23:39Z","created_by":"Test User","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:23:39Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-test-1x4","depends_on_id":"bd-test-ioz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T21:23:54Z","created_by":"Test User","metadata":"{}"}],"dependency_count":1,"dependent_count":0,"comment_count":0}`
17	
18		// DDx must be able to unmarshal bd export without loss of known fields
19		b, err := unmarshalBead([]byte(bdExport))
20		require.NoError(t, err)
21	
22		assert.Equal(t, "bd-test-1x4", b.ID)
23		assert.Equal(t, "test issue", b.Title)
24		assert.Equal(t, "open", b.Status)
25		assert.Equal(t, 2, b.Priority)
26		assert.Equal(t, "task", b.IssueType)
27		assert.Equal(t, "user@example.com", b.Owner)
28		assert.Equal(t, "Test User", b.CreatedBy)
29		assert.False(t, b.CreatedAt.IsZero())
30		assert.False(t, b.UpdatedAt.IsZero())
31	
32		// Dependencies must be parsed
33		require.Len(t, b.Dependencies, 1)
34		assert.Equal(t, "bd-test-1x4", b.Dependencies[0].IssueID)
35		assert.Equal(t, "bd-test-ioz", b.Dependencies[0].DependsOnID)
36		assert.Equal(t, "blocks", b.Dependencies[0].Type)
37	
38		// bd-specific computed fields (dependency_count etc.) should be preserved in Extra
39		assert.Equal(t, float64(1), b.Extra["dependency_count"])
40		assert.Equal(t, float64(0), b.Extra["dependent_count"])
41		assert.Equal(t, float64(0), b.Extra["comment_count"])
42	}
43	
44	// TestBdRoundTrip verifies that a bd-exported bead survives DDx unmarshal→marshal
45	// without losing or renaming any fields.
46	func TestBdRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
47		bdExport := `{"id":"bd-test-abc","title":"Round trip","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"dev@co.com","created_at":"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":"Dev","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:00:00Z","custom_field":"preserved","dependency_count":0}`
48	
49		b, err := unmarshalBead([]byte(bdExport))
50		require.NoError(t, err)
51	
52		// Marshal back
53		out, err := marshalBead(b)
54		require.NoError(t, err)
55	
56		// Parse the output and verify all original fields survive
57		var result map[string]any
58		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(out, &result))
59	
60		// bd-compatible field names
61		assert.Equal(t, "bd-test-abc", result["id"])
62		assert.Equal(t, "Round trip", result["title"])
63		assert.Equal(t, "open", result["status"])
64		assert.Equal(t, float64(1), result["priority"])
65		assert.Equal(t, "epic", result["issue_type"])
66		assert.Equal(t, "dev@co.com", result["owner"])
67		assert.Equal(t, "Dev", result["created_by"])
68	
69		// Custom fields preserved via Extra
70		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", result["custom_field"])
71	
72		// Verify NO old DDx field names leak through
73		assert.Nil(t, result["type"], "must use issue_type, not type")
74		assert.Nil(t, result["assignee"], "must use owner, not assignee")
75		assert.Nil(t, result["created"], "must use created_at, not created")
76		assert.Nil(t, result["updated"], "must use updated_at, not updated")
77		assert.Nil(t, result["deps"], "must use dependencies, not deps")
78	}
79	
80	// TestDdxBeadFieldNames verifies that DDx-created beads use bd-compatible
81	// field names in their JSONL output.
82	func TestDdxBeadFieldNames(t *testing.T) {
83		s := newTestStore(t)
84	
85		b := &Bead{Title: "New bead", IssueType: "task"}
86		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
87	
88		// Marshal and check field names
89		out, err := marshalBead(*b)
90		require.NoError(t, err)
91	
92		var result map[string]any
93		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(out, &result))
94	
95		// Must use bd-compatible names
96		assert.Contains(t, result, "issue_type", "must use issue_type")
97		assert.Contains(t, result, "created_at", "must use created_at")
98		assert.Contains(t, result, "updated_at", "must use updated_at")
99	
100		// Must NOT use old DDx names
101		assert.NotContains(t, result, "type", "must not use 'type' (use issue_type)")
102		assert.NotContains(t, result, "assignee", "must not use 'assignee' (use owner)")
103		assert.NotContains(t, result, "created", "must not use 'created' (use created_at)")
104		assert.NotContains(t, result, "updated", "must not use 'updated' (use updated_at)")
105		assert.NotContains(t, result, "deps", "must not use 'deps' (use dependencies)")
106	}

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"testing"
9	
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
12	)
13	
14	// TestAttachmentClosingExternalizesEvents asserts that closing a bead moves
15	// its inline event history into a sidecar attachment under
16	// .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl and clears the inline copy from the row.
17	//
18	// Acceptance: AC1 (closed beads write events to the attachment store, not
19	// inline) and AC3 (bead-row size shrinks).
20	func TestAttachmentClosingExternalizesEvents(t *testing.T) {
21		s := newTestStore(t)
22	
23		b := &Bead{Title: "to close"}
24		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
25		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "review", Summary: "APPROVE", Body: "looks good"}))
26		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "summary", Summary: "completed"}))
27	
28		// Capture the row size before close so we can demonstrate AC3 shrinkage.
29		beforeRowBytes := len(beadRowBytes(t, s, b.ID))
30	
31		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
32	
33		// Sidecar exists.
34		attPath := s.eventsAttachmentPath(b.ID)
35		info, err := os.Stat(attPath)
36		require.NoError(t, err, "events.jsonl sidecar should exist after close")
37		assert.True(t, info.Size() > 0)
38	
39		// The on-disk row no longer carries inline events; it carries an
40		// attachment ref instead.
41		rowBytes := beadRowBytes(t, s, b.ID)
42		assert.NotContains(t, string(rowBytes), `"events":`)
43		assert.Contains(t, string(rowBytes), `"`+EventsAttachmentExtraKey+`":`)
44	
45		// AC3: the row shrank.
46		assert.Less(t, len(rowBytes), beforeRowBytes,
47			"closed bead row should be smaller after externalization")
48	
49		// Sidecar carries both events with their bodies intact.
50		events, err := s.readEventsAttachment(b.ID)
51		require.NoError(t, err)
52		require.Len(t, events, 2)
53		assert.Equal(t, "review", events[0].Kind)
54		assert.Equal(t, "APPROVE", events[0].Summary)
55		assert.Equal(t, "looks good", events[0].Body)
56		assert.Equal(t, "summary", events[1].Kind)
57	}
58	
59	// TestAttachmentShowReadsTransparently exercises AC2: callers read events
60	// through the normal Events() API regardless of where they live, and the
61	// `--json` show projection inlines events into the marshaled object.
62	func TestAttachmentShowReadsTransparently(t *testing.T) {
63		s := newTestStore(t)
64	
65		b := &Bead{Title: "show me"}
66		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
67		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "model=opus"}))
68		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "review", Summary: "APPROVE", Body: "rationale"}))
69		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
70	
71		// Events() returns the externalized log transparently.
72		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
73		require.NoError(t, err)
74		require.Len(t, events, 2)
75		assert.Equal(t, "routing", events[0].Kind)
76		assert.Equal(t, "review", events[1].Kind)
77	
78		// LoadEventsInline replays the events into Extra["events"] so the JSON
79		// show path projects a single uniform shape.
80		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
81		require.NoError(t, err)
82		require.NotNil(t, got)
83		require.NoError(t, s.LoadEventsInline(got))
84		raw, ok := got.Extra["events"]
85		require.True(t, ok, "LoadEventsInline must populate Extra[events]")
86		inlined := decodeBeadEvents(raw)
87		require.Len(t, inlined, 2)
88		_, attachStillSet := got.Extra[EventsAttachmentExtraKey]
89		assert.False(t, attachStillSet, "in-memory inlining drops the attachment ref")
90	
91		// On-disk row was not modified by the inline operation.
92		rowBytes := beadRowBytes(t, s, b.ID)
93		assert.NotContains(t, string(rowBytes), `"events":`)
94		assert.Contains(t, string(rowBytes), `"`+EventsAttachmentExtraKey+`":`)
95	}
96	
97	// TestAttachmentExportInlinesEvents covers AC4: export emits a single inline
98	// `events` array per row so bd/br interchange survives.
99	func TestAttachmentExportInlinesEvents(t *testing.T) {
100		s := newTestStore(t)
101	
102		b := &Bead{Title: "export me"}
103		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
104		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "model=opus"}))
105		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "review", Summary: "APPROVE", Body: "ok"}))
106		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
107	
108		var buf bytes.Buffer
109		require.NoError(t, s.ExportTo(&buf))
110	
111		out := strings.TrimSpace(buf.String())
112		require.NotEmpty(t, out)
113	
114		var row map[string]any
115		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &row))
116	
117		events, ok := row["events"].([]any)
118		require.True(t, ok, "export must include inline events array; got %v", row["events"])
119		require.Len(t, events, 2)
120		first := events[0].(map[string]any)
121		assert.Equal(t, "routing", first["kind"])
122	
123		// The attachment ref must NOT appear in exported JSON — bd/br doesn't
124		// know how to resolve it and would otherwise see a dangling pointer.
125		_, refStillThere := row[EventsAttachmentExtraKey]
126		assert.False(t, refStillThere, "export must drop the attachment ref")
127	}
128	
129	// TestAttachmentAppendEventAfterCloseAppendsToSidecar covers a subtle case:
130	// once a bead is closed the inline path is gone, so a follow-up AppendEvent
131	// must extend the sidecar rather than re-introducing inline storage.
132	func TestAttachmentAppendEventAfterCloseAppendsToSidecar(t *testing.T) {
133		s := newTestStore(t)
134	
135		b := &Bead{Title: "post-close evt"}
136		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
137		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "first"}))
138		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
139	
140		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "audit", Summary: "post-close"}))
141	
142		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
143		require.NoError(t, err)
144		require.NotNil(t, got)
145		_, hasInline := got.Extra["events"]
146		assert.False(t, hasInline, "post-close append must not re-inline events")
147	
148		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
149		require.NoError(t, err)
150		require.Len(t, events, 2)
151		assert.Equal(t, "audit", events[1].Kind)
152		assert.Equal(t, "post-close", events[1].Summary)
153	}
154	
155	// TestAttachmentReopenInlinesEvents exercises Reopen's promise: a reopened
156	// bead returns to the active queue with its full event history back inline,
157	// the attachment ref cleared, and a fresh "reopen" event appended.
158	func TestAttachmentReopenInlinesEvents(t *testing.T) {
159		s := newTestStore(t)
160	
161		b := &Bead{Title: "reopen me"}
162		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
163		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "first"}))
164		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "review", Summary: "APPROVE", Body: "ok"}))
165		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
166	
167		// Sanity: closed bead has the attachment.
168		require.FileExists(t, s.eventsAttachmentPath(b.ID))
169	
170		require.NoError(t, s.Reopen(b.ID, "needs more work", ""))
171	
172		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
173		require.NoError(t, err)
174		require.NotNil(t, got)
175		assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
176		_, attachStillSet := got.Extra[EventsAttachmentExtraKey]
177		assert.False(t, attachStillSet, "reopen must drop the attachment ref")
178	
179		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
180		require.NoError(t, err)
181		require.Len(t, events, 3, "two prior events plus the reopen event")
182		assert.Equal(t, "reopen", events[2].Kind)
183		assert.Equal(t, "needs more work", events[2].Summary)
184	
185		// The sidecar file is removed once events are inlined back.
186		_, statErr := os.Stat(s.eventsAttachmentPath(b.ID))
187		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr),
188			"sidecar should be deleted after reopen inlines events")
189	}
190	
191	// TestAttachmentClosureGateAcceptsAttachedEvents guards against the gate
192	// rejecting a follow-up close on a bead whose events live in the sidecar
193	// (the gate's existing check only inspected Extra[events]).
194	func TestAttachmentClosureGateAcceptsAttachedEvents(t *testing.T) {
195		b := &Bead{
196			ID: "ddx-gate-att",
197			Extra: map[string]any{
198				EventsAttachmentExtraKey: "ddx-gate-att/events.jsonl",
199				"closing_commit_sha":     "deadbeef",
200			},
201		}
202		// No inline events, but the attachment ref + closing commit together
203		// must satisfy the gate (the inline-only path would otherwise reject).
204		assert.NoError(t, ClosureGate(b))
205	}
206	
207	// beadRowBytes returns the raw JSONL line for the given bead ID from the
208	// store's primary file, so tests can compare row sizes before/after
209	// externalization (AC3).
210	func beadRowBytes(t *testing.T, s *Store, id string) []byte {
211		t.Helper()
212		data, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
213		require.NoError(t, err)
214		for _, line := range bytes.Split(data, []byte("\n")) {
215			line = bytes.TrimSpace(line)
216			if len(line) == 0 {
217				continue
218			}
219			var m map[string]any
220			if err := json.Unmarshal(line, &m); err != nil {
221				continue
222			}
223			if rid, _ := m["id"].(string); rid == id {
224				return line
225			}
226		}
227		t.Fatalf("bead %s not found in %s", id, s.File)
228		return nil
229	}

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"time"
6	)
7	
8	// BeadsArchiveCollection is the logical collection name for archived beads.
9	// It is registered in the default registry alongside the active "beads"
10	// collection and is backed by .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl in the JSONL backend.
11	const BeadsArchiveCollection = "beads-archive"
12	
13	// ArchivePolicy parameterises which closed beads are eligible to move from
14	// the active collection to the archive. Defaults match TD-027 §(b).
15	type ArchivePolicy struct {
16		// Statuses lists the bead statuses that are eligible for archival.
17		Statuses []string
18		// MinAge is the minimum time since a bead was last updated/closed
19		// before it may be archived.
20		MinAge time.Duration
21		// MinActiveCount is the floor on active-collection size below which
22		// archival is a no-op. Set to 0 to archive regardless of size.
23		MinActiveCount int
24		// BatchSize caps how many records move per call.
25		BatchSize int
26	}
27	
28	// DefaultArchivePolicy returns the shipping defaults from TD-027 §(b).
29	func DefaultArchivePolicy() ArchivePolicy {
30		return ArchivePolicy{
31			Statuses:       []string{StatusClosed},
32			MinAge:         30 * 24 * time.Hour,
33			MinActiveCount: 2000,
34			BatchSize:      500,
35		}
36	}
37	
38	// archivePartner returns a Store for the beads-archive collection rooted at
39	// the same .ddx directory as s. The partner reuses the shipping registry
40	// spec for "beads-archive" so file/lock paths are uniform.
41	func (s *Store) archivePartner() *Store {
42		return NewStoreWithCollection(s.Dir, BeadsArchiveCollection)
43	}
44	
45	// Archive moves eligible closed beads from this active store to the
46	// beads-archive partner. It applies preserve_dependencies semantics from
47	// TD-027 §(b): a closed bead that an open or in_progress bead still
48	// references is retained in the active collection so SD-004 queue
49	// derivation can resolve it without loading the archive.
50	//
51	// Returns the IDs that were moved.
52	func (s *Store) Archive(policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error) {
53		if s.Collection != DefaultCollection {
54			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: archive only runs from the active %q collection (got %q)", DefaultCollection, s.Collection)
55		}
56		archive := s.archivePartner()
57	
58		var moved []string
59		// Lock order is fixed: active first, archive second, per TD-027 §(b).
60		err := s.WithLock(func() error {
61			return archive.WithLock(func() error {
62				activeBeads, _, rerr := s.readAllLatestRaw()
63				if rerr != nil {
64					return rerr
65				}
66				if policy.MinActiveCount > 0 && len(activeBeads) < policy.MinActiveCount {
67					return nil
68				}
69	
70				// preserve_dependencies: skip closed beads still referenced by
71				// any non-closed bead's dependency list.
72				referenced := make(map[string]bool)
73				for _, b := range activeBeads {
74					if b.Status == StatusClosed {
75						continue
76					}
77					for _, dep := range b.DepIDs() {
78						referenced[dep] = true
79					}
80				}

data.text:
Committed. AC summary:

1. ✅ `cli/internal/bead/schema_compat_test.go` passes (unchanged, verified).
2. ✅ `ddx bead export` now streams archived bead events inline — extended `ExportTo` in `cli/internal/bead/import.go` to merge archive partner with attachment-resolved events.
3. ✅ Round-trip verified in new test: each exported line re-parses via `unmarshalBead` (bd consumer simulation).
4. ✅ New test `TestExportStreamsArchiveAndAttachmentEvents` in `cli/internal/bead/attachments_test.go` covers the migrate→export archive+attachment path.
